text.title: Critical-Issue Triage Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Critical-Issue Triage Protocol. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each finding is assigned severity 0..4 using the calibrated rubric (0 cosmetic / 1 minor / 2 major / 3 catastrophic / 4 catastrophic+accessibility-safety); rating ad-hoc adjectives are rejected.
rule.rationale: Consistent 0..4 scale enables trend analysis across sessions; ad-hoc severity makes triage incomparable.

rule.statement: Frequency is expressed as a band against observed N: SINGLE (1/N), FEW (2-3/N or <=30%), MANY (40-70%), MOST (>70%). Raw counts without a band are rejected.
rule.rationale: With small N, a single occurrence may already signal a real population issue. Bands acknowledge sample limits without dismissing severe one-offs.

rule.statement: Context modifiers are applied: REVENUE-CRITICAL +1, REGULATED +1, INTERNAL-TOOL -1. The modifier is recorded so the rationale is auditable.
rule.rationale: Impact varies by path criticality; the modifier captures the business context raw severity ignores.

rule.statement: Action is taken from the matrix lookup (severity × frequency × context-modifier → SAME-DAY-FIX / NEXT-SPRINT / BACKLOG / DISCARD). Overrides require a written one-line justification.
rule.rationale: Deterministic lookup prevents the loud-finding bias — a vivid finding gets escalated past its real severity.

rule.statement: Each triage row carries a one-line evidence statement (e.g. 'user 3 quit after 7 attempts') alongside the score; rows without evidence are rejected.
rule.rationale: Score-without-evidence is folklore; the evidence line lets reviewers re-litigate the decision when needed.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-critical-issue-triage-protocol.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
